description: A plain screenshot of a U.S. Department of Justice / U.S. Attorney's Office (Southern District of Ohio) web page, captured from a browser. At the top is a dark navy header bar with the DOJ seal and the masthead 'United States Attorney's Office / Southern District of Ohio,' a search box, and a navigation menu (About, News, Divisions, Case Updates, Programs, Espanol). A breadcrumb trail reads 'Justice.gov > U.S. Attorneys > Southern District of Ohio > Press Releases.' The body is a standard press-release layout in serif/sans-serif body text on white, labeled 'PRESS RELEASE' with the headline 'Grand jury indicts man who allegedly made death threats against ICE agents,' dated 'Wednesday, August 13, 2025,' with a 'For Immediate Release' sidebar. The article text describes Anthony Marcus Kelly, 38, of Cincinnati, charged in a two-count indictment for making threats to assault or murder a U.S. official and threatening interstate communications, quoting his alleged social-media posts ('#Gestapedos,' 'they're rabid dogs that need to be put down,' 'You come here for me, you're getting shot') and noting penalties of up to 10 and 5 years. This is a routine document/webpage capture with no custom graphic design beyond the standard government website template, so it is not a designed meme. ICE shared it to publicize prosecution of someone who threatened ICE officers, signaling deterrence to a general/online audience.
notable_text: Grand jury indicts man who allegedly made death threats against ICE agents | Threatening to assault or murder a United States official is a federal crime punishable by up to 10 years in prison.
